message:
fix(h2): wire role-aware settings into connection and reject server ENABLE_PUSH

Completes the RFC 7540 §6.5.2 compliance work from the prior settings.rs
commit by integrating role-awareness into the Connection layer:

Server-side: queue_initial_settings() now calls
to_settings_minimal_for_role(self.is_client) instead of the role-unaware
to_settings_minimal(), ensuring server connections never emit
SETTINGS_ENABLE_PUSH in their initial SETTINGS frame.

Client-side: process_settings_frame() now rejects incoming
Setting::EnablePush from the remote peer when self.is_client is true,
returning a ProtocolError. This guards against non-compliant servers that
incorrectly include ENABLE_PUSH in their SETTINGS frames.

Adds three targeted tests:
- client_rejects_server_enable_push_setting: verifies ProtocolError is
  returned and no ACK is queued for the invalid frame
- server_initial_settings_omit_enable_push: verifies server connections
  never serialize EnablePush in their initial settings
- existing client test updated to assert EnablePush(false) is present
  in client initial settings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
